title: OpenAI chat non-stream ignores the signed token allowed_models caveat
source: Authenticated caller controls the inbound model string while presenting a valid signed TrustToken whose allowed_models excludes that model.
closest_control: resolve_and_check verifies signature/expiry/optional revocation and returns the token; ModelMap resolves the caller string. Neither authorize nor resolve_provider compares the inbound/target model with ctx.token.allowed_models.
sink_or_broken_control: OpenAI chat non-stream dispatches provider.complete/stream after model-map resolution without token model authorization.
impact: A restricted virtual key can invoke an unauthorized configured model/provider, consuming privileged or higher-cost inference capacity and crossing the token's attenuation boundary.
plausibility: Repository search found allowed_models enforcement only in token attenuation construction, not in the AOG gateway request path; the wrapper and concrete provider sink are directly connected.

validation.method: static full-chain trace plus repository symbol search
validation.evidence: confirmed: valid token context reaches the concrete sink with no allowed_models read in aog-gateway
validation.proof_gap: No runtime harness was added; target code is read-only.

attack_path.attacker: holder of any otherwise valid virtual key
preconditions: requested model is configured or accepted by ModelMap fallback
signed token allowed_models does not authorize it
attack_path.closest_control_failure: model mapping is configuration routing, not subject authorization
severity_facts: network reachable behind a valid key
crosses a signed token capability axis

title: OpenAI chat non-stream ignores the signed token allowed_routes caveat
source: Authenticated caller uses a token restricted to local routes and selects a configured cloud-backed model with benign content.
closest_control: classify_and_route and PolicyEngine gate content sensitivity, but no code intersects the selected/effective route with ctx.token.allowed_routes.
sink_or_broken_control: OpenAI chat non-stream can dispatch to a cloud provider after content policy allows it even when the signed token has no CloudAllowed authority.
impact: A local-only token can cause cloud egress and provider use, defeating token attenuation and sovereign routing policy.
plausibility: The TrustToken route caveat is returned in ResolvedContext but is never read by aog-gateway; the concrete route decision and provider sink are directly connected.

validation.method: static full-chain trace plus repository symbol search
validation.evidence: confirmed: no allowed_routes enforcement exists between verified token and dispatch
validation.proof_gap: No runtime harness was added; target code is read-only.

attack_path.attacker: holder of a valid route-restricted virtual key
preconditions: configured cloud target
benign text receives CloudAllowed from router/policy
attack_path.closest_control_failure: content classification does not enforce token route authority
severity_facts: valid-key prerequisite
can violate local-first and signed attenuation invariants

title: OpenAI chat non-stream uses non-reserving budget preflight and can overspend
source: Authenticated caller controls request size/max_tokens and can issue concurrent requests under the same attenuation lineage.
closest_control: The preflight folds already-recorded spend and rejects only when current spend is already at/over cap. It neither reserves requested capacity nor atomically couples admission to settlement.
sink_or_broken_control: OpenAI chat non-stream reaches a provider before usage is added; one request may exceed the remaining cap and concurrent admissions can all observe the same remaining balance.
impact: A tenant can exceed token/USD budget caps, incur cloud cost, and exhaust inference capacity despite a signed budget restriction.
plausibility: The code comment itself notes the lease-based reserve flow is not adopted; settlement is post-response/post-stream while preflight is a separate fold/check.

validation.method: static interleaving and single-request bound analysis
validation.evidence: confirmed: check and add are separate; no requested-usage comparison exists. Existing tests explicitly allow a first stream to cross its cap and only reject the next call.
validation.proof_gap: No concurrent runtime stress harness was run.

attack_path.attacker: holder of a valid budgeted virtual key
preconditions: remaining budget is positive at preflight
steps: submit a request whose eventual usage exceeds remaining budget or race several requests
attack_path.closest_control_failure: current-balance check is not a reservation/commit protocol
attack_path.impact_path: budget bypass, cloud charges, and resource exhaustion
severity_facts: ordinary tenant access
single-request overshoot is deterministic; concurrency amplifies it

title: OpenAI chat non-stream executes on a local target after an explicit router Denied decision
source: Caller submits text that the configured Router returns as RoutingDecision::Denied.
closest_control: classify_and_route preserves denied=true, but apply_mode defines violations only as target_is_cloud && effective_route==LocalOnly and never checks decision.route.denied.
sink_or_broken_control: When the mapped target is local, OpenAI chat non-stream continues to provider execution despite the router contract saying Reject the request entirely.
impact: Content subject to a hard deny policy is processed locally, bypassing the router's explicit rejection and any policy semantics attached to that deny.
plausibility: The shared Denied branch and apply_mode omission are explicit; mai-router documents Denied as reject entirely and DefaultRouter produces it at the configured deny classification floor.

validation.method: static branch trace with router contract inspection
validation.evidence: confirmed for a local target: denied flag is set, gate returns Ok because target_cloud is false, then concrete provider sink executes
validation.proof_gap: No request harness was added to force DefaultRouter's deny classification.

attack_path.attacker: authenticated inference caller
preconditions: query triggers RoutingDecision::Denied
selected model maps to provider named local
steps: Denied is converted to LocalOnly plus marker
gate treats local dispatch as non-violation
attack_path.closest_control_failure: deny marker is audit-only, not enforcement
severity_facts: valid-key prerequisite
local processing only, but explicit policy control is defeated

title: OpenAI chat non-stream accepts current-snapshot-revoked tokens under the default Gateway configuration
source: Caller presents an otherwise valid, unexpired token that is revoked only in the current signed RevocationSnapshot.
closest_control: fabric_token::verify rejects an embedded Revoked status, and configured snapshots are verified/freshness-checked; however Gateway::new defaults revocation_kv_path to empty and AppState::new Production does not require enabling it.
sink_or_broken_control: OpenAI chat non-stream skips snapshot lookup entirely when the default empty path is retained and proceeds to provider execution.
impact: A killed token, subject, tenant, lineage, service, identity, or trust-bundle version can continue inference until expiry in a default-constructed production-profile application.
plausibility: Default constructor and conditional are direct; package tests and normal AppState construction use Gateway::new without any production invariant that revocation must be configured.

validation.method: static configuration/path trace
validation.evidence: confirmed for Gateway::new without with_revocation_path: the only current snapshot block is skipped
validation.proof_gap: No authoritative shipped binary wiring exists in this crate, so final deployment prevalence must be confirmed by the parent scan.

attack_path.attacker: holder of a token revoked after issuance
preconditions: application uses default empty revocation path
token remains signed/unexpired and embedded status is not Revoked
attack_path.closest_control_failure: revocation is opt-in even though AppState defaults to Production
severity_facts: network reachable with revoked credential
deployment precondition is the default constructor

validation.evidence: confirmed: check and add are separate; no requested-usage comparison exists. Existing tests explicitly allow a first stream to cross its cap and only reject the next call.
validation.proof_gap: No concurrent runtime stress harness was run.

attack_path.attacker: holder of a valid budgeted virtual key
preconditions: remaining budget is positive at preflight
steps: submit a request whose eventual usage exceeds remaining budget or race several requests
attack_path.closest_control_failure: current-balance check is not a reservation/commit protocol
attack_path.impact_path: budget bypass, cloud charges, and resource exhaustion
severity_facts: ordinary tenant access
single-request overshoot is deterministic; concurrency amplifies it

title: Anthropic message SSE executes on a local target after an explicit router Denied decision
source: Caller submits text that the configured Router returns as RoutingDecision::Denied.
closest_control: classify_and_route preserves denied=true, but apply_mode defines violations only as target_is_cloud && effective_route==LocalOnly and never checks decision.route.denied.
sink_or_broken_control: When the mapped target is local, Anthropic message SSE continues to provider execution despite the router contract saying Reject the request entirely.
impact: Content subject to a hard deny policy is processed locally, bypassing the router's explicit rejection and any policy semantics attached to that deny.
plausibility: The shared Denied branch and apply_mode omission are explicit; mai-router documents Denied as reject entirely and DefaultRouter produces it at the configured deny classification floor.

validation.method: static branch trace with router contract inspection
validation.evidence: confirmed for a local target: denied flag is set, gate returns Ok because target_cloud is false, then concrete provider sink executes
validation.proof_gap: No request harness was added to force DefaultRouter's deny classification.

attack_path.attacker: authenticated inference caller
preconditions: query triggers RoutingDecision::Denied
selected model maps to provider named local
steps: Denied is converted to LocalOnly plus marker
gate treats local dispatch as non-violation
attack_path.closest_control_failure: deny marker is audit-only, not enforcement
severity_facts: valid-key prerequisite
local processing only, but explicit policy control is defeated

title: Anthropic message SSE accepts current-snapshot-revoked tokens under the default Gateway configuration
source: Caller presents an otherwise valid, unexpired token that is revoked only in the current signed RevocationSnapshot.
closest_control: fabric_token::verify rejects an embedded Revoked status, and configured snapshots are verified/freshness-checked; however Gateway::new defaults revocation_kv_path to empty and AppState::new Production does not require enabling it.
sink_or_broken_control: Anthropic message SSE skips snapshot lookup entirely when the default empty path is retained and proceeds to provider execution.
impact: A killed token, subject, tenant, lineage, service, identity, or trust-bundle version can continue inference until expiry in a default-constructed production-profile application.
plausibility: Default constructor and conditional are direct; package tests and normal AppState construction use Gateway::new without any production invariant that revocation must be configured.

validation.method: static configuration/path trace
validation.evidence: confirmed for Gateway::new without with_revocation_path: the only current snapshot block is skipped
validation.proof_gap: No authoritative shipped binary wiring exists in this crate, so final deployment prevalence must be confirmed by the parent scan.

attack_path.attacker: holder of a token revoked after issuance
preconditions: application uses default empty revocation path
token remains signed/unexpired and embedded status is not Revoked
attack_path.closest_control_failure: revocation is opt-in even though AppState defaults to Production
severity_facts: network reachable with revoked credential
deployment precondition is the default constructor

title: Exported envelope-label routing trusts an unverified label
source: A caller of the public package API can supply a deserialized Envelope with attacker-chosen classification and permitted_destinations.
closest_control: fabric-envelope exposes verify_thread to authenticate seal+label+authorizing token, but route_from_envelope calls only read_label and its type/signature does not require prior verification evidence.
sink_or_broken_control: tighten can return CloudAllowed from an unauthenticated label, and route_from_envelope marks it EnvelopeLabel without reclassification.
impact: If a runtime consumer uses this exported flagship API on boundary input, forged labels can steer regulated content to cloud egress.
plausibility: The trust mismatch is concrete at the package API, but repository search found no production call site beyond tests in the scanned snapshot.

validation.method: static API and call-site search
validation.evidence: confirmed that label is read without verify_thread
validation.proof_gap: No deployed/runtime caller of route_from_envelope was found, so network reachability and actual egress remain unproven.

attack_path.attacker: supplier of an unverified Envelope to a downstream package consumer
preconditions: a runtime consumer calls route_from_envelope before verify_thread and uses the result for dispatch
steps: helper trusts label and skips reclassification
attack_path.closest_control_failure: verification is available but not required or called
severity_facts: package API is exported
no current in-repository runtime caller

title: Public preflight amplifies arbitrary bearer strings into OpenBao login and KV traffic
source: Unauthenticated network caller supplies any nonempty syntactically valid Bearer value to POST /v1/preflight.
closest_control: bearer_key validates only header shape. The router has no rate, concurrency, or negative-credential cache layer before resolve_and_check performs OpenBao login and KV lookup.
sink_or_broken_control: Each junk credential triggers OpenBaoAuth::login and get_kv_data before UnknownKey can be returned.
impact: Remote callers can amplify cheap requests into trust-store authentication and KV work, degrading gateway/OpenBao availability and potentially starving legitimate authorization.
plausibility: The package router mounts the route directly and the full source-to-external-service chain is explicit; outer deployment middleware could reduce reachability but is not required by this API.

validation.method: static entrypoint-to-OpenBao trace
validation.evidence: confirmed: arbitrary nonempty bearer reaches login at lib.rs:144 and KV read at 147 before unknown-key handling
validation.proof_gap: No load test was run and parent scan should confirm whether all shipped deployments wrap this Router in a limiter.

attack_path.attacker: unauthenticated remote client
preconditions: preflight router is network exposed without an outer limiter
steps: send many requests with distinct junk bearer values
attack_path.closest_control_failure: authentication backend work occurs before any request throttling or negative caching
severity_facts: no valid key required
external OpenBao dependency multiplies work
deployment middleware may lower exploitability
